INTERVIEWER: How was the week?

INTERVIEWEE: Mixed. I got the dashboard out, that was the big one. I also lost most of Thursday because GCP threw a billing alert and I had to spend the day un-fucking the project setup.

INTERVIEWER: What part of the dashboard shipped?

INTERVIEWEE: The metrics tiles and the date range filter. I didn't get the export-to-CSV in. Users have been asking for it. They keep filing it as a P0 in support but I had to push it to next week because of the GCP thing.

INTERVIEWER: Was the GCP issue on you or external?

INTERVIEWEE: Both. I had set up billing on a personal card three months ago because I was lazy. So when the team account got linked, GCP flagged it as a payment-method change and shut down the project for review. So they triggered it but I made the mess that let them trigger it.

INTERVIEWER: That's clear. What else this week?

INTERVIEWEE: I had three user calls. Two of them were great, very specific feedback about the onboarding wording. One was a guy who I think wasn't really a prospect, he was just looking around. I should have qualified him before I took the call.

INTERVIEWER: How would you qualify next time?

INTERVIEWEE: A short async form. Three questions. If they can't fill that out it's not worth me showing up live. I'll set that up Monday.

INTERVIEWER: What about the team — anything moving there?

INTERVIEWEE: My co-founder is wrestling with the back end migration. He's been quiet about it. I should check in with him properly, not just on standup. He gets stuck in his head when things are hard. That's on me to notice earlier.

INTERVIEWER: When was the last time you and he had an actual conversation, not a standup?

INTERVIEWEE: Probably ten days ago. We've both been heads-down. That's not great.

INTERVIEWER: What will you do about it?

INTERVIEWEE: Tomorrow morning. I'll suggest a walk. He likes walks. We'll talk about the migration but also just how he's doing.

INTERVIEWER: Good. Milestone check?

INTERVIEWEE: Behind on revenue, on track on product. We said ten paying by end of month. We have four. We said dashboard MVP shipped. That's basically done, minus the CSV export.

INTERVIEWER: What's the most likely path to ten?

INTERVIEWEE: Honestly, asking the four current ones for referrals. They love it. I haven't asked because I felt weird about it. That's a me thing — I should just ask.
